title: Workbook — The Eucharist
html:
Student Workbook

For use after class or in a group. Print this page or work in your notebook.

Remembering the Essentials

  1. Why does the Catechism call the Eucharist «the source and summit of the Christian life» (CCC 1324)? What does that phrase mean?
  2. What is transubstantiation? Explain it in your own words, without using technical terms.
  3. What are the four New Testament narratives of the institution of the Eucharist?
  4. Name at least three Old Testament prefigurations of the Eucharist and briefly explain each one.
  5. What are the five fruits of Communion that the Catechism identifies?

For Reflection and Writing

Answer each question honestly. There are no «right» answers to these personal questions.

1. What does it mean to you, personally, that Jesus Christ is really present in the consecrated host?

Do you truly believe it, or do you simply accept it because the Church says so? Be honest with yourself.

Write your answer here (4-6 lines):

 

 

 

 

 

2. What would change in your life if, starting this Sunday, you participated in Mass with full awareness of being at the foot of the cross?

Think about three moments: before Mass (preparation), during Mass (attention and attitude), and after Mass (life).

Before:

 

 

During:

 

 

After:

 

 

3. Of the seven obstacles to recognizing God that the Catechism identifies (CCC 29), which do you think most keeps people away from the Eucharist today?

Think about the people you know who don't go to Mass. Why don't they? Indifference, ignorance, bad example, other priorities...?

 

 

 

 

4. Is there anything in Jesus' teaching about the Eucharist (Jn 6) that you find hard to accept or live out? Why?

Many abandoned Jesus at Capernaum. Peter stayed. Where are you? What keeps you close?

 

 

 

 

5. The Cristeros died for the Eucharist. You have it available every Sunday (and every day) just minutes from home. What does that contrast say to you?

If Mass were banned tomorrow, would you risk anything to attend? What would you be willing to lose?

 

 

 

 

Practical Activities

A. Prayerful Reading of John 6

Read John 6:22-69 in one sitting, slowly. Underline with one color the phrases that strike you. With another color, those you don't understand. Then write:

Three phrases that illuminate you:

1. _______________________________________________

2. _______________________________________________

3. _______________________________________________

One question you would ask Jesus about this text:

 

 

B. Visit to the Blessed Sacrament

Find a church with a tabernacle (every Catholic church has one). Go in, kneel down, and stay in silence for fifteen minutes. You don't need to say anything. Just keep Jesus company, as you would visit a friend who is waiting for you. St. Manuel González said: «Stay with Him for a while. You won't regret it.»

Afterwards, write: What did you feel, think, or experience?

 

 

 

 

 

C. Mass with Intention

At the next Mass you attend, don't just go «to fulfill an obligation.» Prepare your heart:

  1. Before: Choose a concrete intention for which to offer the Mass (for someone who is suffering, a problem, a thanksgiving). If possible, read the day's readings before arriving.
  2. During: Listen to the readings as if God were speaking to you personally. At the consecration, look at the host and say interiorly: «My Lord and my God» (Jn 20:28).
  3. After: Stay a few minutes in thanksgiving. Don't rush out. Thank Him for remaining in you.

What was different this time?

 

 

 

 

D. Map of the Names of the Eucharist

The names the Church gives to this sacrament reveal its richness. Complete this chart by matching each name with its meaning:

NameMeaning
Eucharist_____________________________
Holy Mass_____________________________
Communion_____________________________
Holy Sacrifice_____________________________
Breaking of Bread_____________________________
Viaticum_____________________________

Verse to Memorize

«I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats of this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give is my flesh for the life of the world.»

— John 6:51

Prayer for This Week

Lord Jesus, present in the Eucharist,
I thank you because you did not stay far away,
but became bread to enter into me.
Forgive me for the times I received Communion out of routine,
without thinking about Whom I was receiving.
Awaken in me the faith of Peter:
«Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.»
Give me the love of St. Tarcisius, who chose death rather than lose You.
Give me the passion of St. Manuel González,
who could not bear to see You alone in the tabernacle.
And give me the courage of the Cristeros,
who gave their lives for the Mass.
May the Eucharist truly be, from this day forward,
the center of my life.
Amen.

Print Workbook
